content.text: Token streaming sends each token to the client as the model generates it, reducing perceived latency from seconds to milliseconds. Instead of waiting for the full response, the user sees words appear incrementally — the same experience as Claude.ai. For agentic apps, streaming also enables real-time display of reasoning steps and tool calls as they happen.

attrs.alt: Token Streaming Pipeline — Claude API → FastAPI SSE → Browser EventSource → UI

content.text: The Anthropic Python SDK exposes a context-manager-based streaming API. Use

import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

def stream_response(messages: list[dict], model: str = "claude-sonnet-5") -> None:
    """Print tokens to stdout as they arrive."""
    with client.messages.stream(
        model=model,
        max_tokens=1024,
        messages=messages,
    ) as stream:
        for text in stream.text_stream:
            print(text, end="", flush=True)
        print()  # newline after completion

    # Access the final message after the stream closes
    final = stream.get_final_message()
    print(f"\nInput tokens: {final.usage.input_tokens}")
    print(f"Output tokens: {final.usage.output_tokens}")
yields only the concatenated text deltas — the easiest way to get streaming text. For tool use and other event types, iterate
block ensures the underlying HTTP connection is properly closed even if you break out early.
blocks until the stream finishes and returns the complete accumulated
For agentic loops that include tool use, you need to process multiple event types from the stream — text deltas, tool calls starting, input JSON accumulating, and stop reasons.

import anthropic, json

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

def stream_with_tools(
    messages: list[dict],
    tools: list[dict],
) -> tuple[str, list[dict]]:
    """Stream a response and collect both text and tool use blocks."""
    accumulated_text = ""
    tool_blocks: list[dict] = []
    current_tool: dict | None = None
    current_json_buf = ""

    with client.messages.stream(
        model="claude-sonnet-5",
        max_tokens=4096,
        messages=messages,
        tools=tools,
    ) as stream:
        for event in stream:
            match event.type:
                case "content_block_start":
                    if event.content_block.type == "tool_use":
                        current_tool = {
                            "type": "tool_use",
                            "id": event.content_block.id,
                            "name": event.content_block.name,
                            "input": {},
                        }
                        current_json_buf = ""

                case "content_block_delta":
                    delta = event.delta
                    if delta.type == "text_delta":
                        accumulated_text += delta.text
                        # Yield to UI here in a real async generator
                        print(delta.text, end="", flush=True)
                    elif delta.type == "input_json_delta":
                        current_json_buf += delta.partial_json

                case "content_block_stop":
                    if current_tool is not None:
                        current_tool["input"] = json.loads(current_json_buf)
                        tool_blocks.append(current_tool)
                        current_tool = None
                        current_json_buf = ""

                case "message_stop":
                    pass  # stream.get_final_message() has full details

    return accumulated_text, tool_blocks
Serving Token Streams via Server-Sent Events
Server-Sent Events (SSE) is the standard browser protocol for streaming text. FastAPI's

import asyncio, json
import anthropic
from fastapi import FastAPI, Request
from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse

app = FastAPI()
client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic()

@app.post("/chat/stream")
async def chat_stream(request: Request, body: dict):
    messages = body["messages"]
    model = body.get("model", "claude-sonnet-5")

    async def event_generator():
        try:
            async with client.messages.stream(
                model=model,
                max_tokens=4096,
                messages=messages,
            ) as stream:
                async for text in stream.text_stream:
                    # Check if client disconnected between tokens
                    if await request.is_disconnected():
                        break
                    # SSE format: each event is "data: <payload>\n\n"
                    payload = json.dumps({"type": "token", "text": text})
                    yield f"data: {payload}\n\n"

            # Signal completion
            yield f"data: {json.dumps({'type': 'done'})}\n\n"

        except asyncio.CancelledError:
            # Client disconnected — clean exit
            yield f"data: {json.dumps({'type': 'cancelled'})}\n\n"

    return StreamingResponse(
        event_generator(),
        media_type="text/event-stream",
        headers={
            "Cache-Control": "no-cache",
            "X-Accel-Buffering": "no",  # disable nginx buffering
        },
    )
without this header, nginx buffers SSE events and the client receives them in batches, defeating the purpose of streaming.
required by the SSE specification; tells proxies not to cache event streams.
on each token adds minimal overhead but prevents the server from burning tokens on a gone client.
is simpler but only supports GET; use
Streaming with asyncio Queue and Backpressure
When the stream feeds multiple consumers (e.g., both the UI and a logging sink), use an
provides backpressure — if the consumer is slow, the producer blocks rather than flooding memory.

import asyncio
import anthropic

client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic()

async def stream_to_queue(
    messages: list[dict],
    queue: asyncio.Queue,
    abort: asyncio.Event,
) -> None:
    """Producer: puts tokens into queue; stops on abort signal."""
    try:
        async with client.messages.stream(
            model="claude-sonnet-5",
            max_tokens=2048,
            messages=messages,
        ) as stream:
            async for text in stream.text_stream:
                if abort.is_set():
                    break
                await queue.put({"type": "token", "text": text})
    finally:
        await queue.put({"type": "done"})

async def consume_queue(queue: asyncio.Queue, output: list[str]) -> None:
    """Consumer: reads tokens from queue and appends to output list."""
    while True:
        item = await queue.get()
        if item["type"] == "done":
            break
        output.append(item["text"])
        queue.task_done()

async def run_with_backpressure(messages: list[dict]) -> str:
    queue: asyncio.Queue = asyncio.Queue(maxsize=20)  # buffer up to 20 tokens
    abort = asyncio.Event()
    output: list[str] = []

    await asyncio.gather(
        stream_to_queue(messages, queue, abort),
        consume_queue(queue, output),
    )
    return "".join(output)
When Claude calls a tool with a large JSON argument (e.g., a code block to write to a file), the input arrives as a stream of partial JSON fragments via
events. Accumulate these fragments and parse only at
Append to text buffer, yield to UI

In a multi-turn agentic loop, streaming must survive the tool execution round-trip. The pattern is: stream text, collect tool calls, execute tools synchronously, then open a new stream for the continuation.

import asyncio
import anthropic
from typing import AsyncIterator

client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic()

async def agentic_stream_loop(
    messages: list[dict],
    tools: list[dict],
    tool_executor,  # async callable(name, input) -> dict
) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
    """
    Yield text tokens to the UI while handling tool calls internally.
    Usage: async for token in agentic_stream_loop(...): send(token)
    """
    while True:
        text_parts: list[str] = []
        tool_calls: list[dict] = []
        stop_reason = None

        async with client.messages.stream(
            model="claude-sonnet-5",
            max_tokens=4096,
            messages=messages,
            tools=tools,
        ) as stream:
            async for event in stream:
                if event.type == "content_block_delta":
                    if event.delta.type == "text_delta":
                        text_parts.append(event.delta.text)
                        yield event.delta.text  # <-- streamed to UI immediately

            final = await stream.get_final_message()
            stop_reason = final.stop_reason
            # Rebuild tool_calls from final.content for correctness
            for block in final.content:
                if block.type == "tool_use":
                    tool_calls.append({
                        "type": "tool_use",
                        "id": block.id,
                        "name": block.name,
                        "input": block.input,
                    })

        if stop_reason != "tool_use" or not tool_calls:
            break

        # Append assistant message and execute tools
        messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": final.content})

        tool_results = await asyncio.gather(*[
            tool_executor(tc["name"], tc["input"])
            for tc in tool_calls
        ])

        messages.append({
            "role": "user",
            "content": [
                {
                    "type": "tool_result",
                    "tool_use_id": tc["id"],
                    "content": str(result),
                }
                for tc, result in zip(tool_calls, tool_results)
            ],
        })
        # Loop continues — next turn streams the continuation
inside the stream context to give the UI tokens as they arrive, not as a batch after tool execution.
reconstruct tool calls from the accumulated
rather than tracking deltas manually — the SDK handles JSON fragment concatenation.
the UI sees a pause during tool execution. Signal this by yielding a sentinel event (e.g.,
) before tool calls so the UI can show a spinner.
Streams can fail mid-generation due to API errors, network timeouts, or context window exhaustion. Wrap the stream in try/except blocks and emit a structured error event to the client.

import anthropic
from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse
import json

async def safe_stream_generator(messages, model, request):
    try:
        async with client.messages.stream(
            model=model,
            max_tokens=4096,
            messages=messages,
        ) as stream:
            async for text in stream.text_stream:
                if await request.is_disconnected():
                    return
                yield f"data: {json.dumps({'type': 'token', 'text': text})}\n\n"
            yield f"data: {json.dumps({'type': 'done'})}\n\n"

    except anthropic.BadRequestError as e:
        # Context window exceeded or invalid request
        error_msg = "Context limit reached. Please start a new conversation."
        yield f"data: {json.dumps({'type': 'error', 'message': error_msg})}\n\n"

    except anthropic.APIStatusError as e:
        if e.status_code == 529:  # Overloaded
            yield f"data: {json.dumps({'type': 'error', 'message': 'API overloaded, retry in a moment.'})}\n\n"
        else:
            yield f"data: {json.dumps({'type': 'error', 'message': f'API error {e.status_code}'})}\n\n"

    except Exception as e:
        yield f"data: {json.dumps({'type': 'error', 'message': 'Unexpected error occurred.'})}\n\n"
Always emit a done or error event:
if the generator exits without sending one of these, the client may hang waiting for the stream to close.
often means the message array exceeds the model context window. Truncate older messages and retry rather than propagating a raw 400 to the user.
log the full exception server-side; send only a sanitised message string to the client.
Token streaming requires three components to work together: the
generator that formats each token as an SSE frame; and a
(EventSource or fetch ReadableStream) that appends tokens to the DOM. Poll
on each token to stop wasting compute when a client navigates away. For tool-use responses, accumulate
fragments in a per-block buffer and parse the JSON only at
with a bounded maxsize for backpressure when the consumer is slower than the producer.
